name: The Nightmare Before Christmas
description: The 1993 Tim Burton-produced, Henry Selick-directed stop-motion cult classic

questions.q: Who directed The Nightmare Before Christmas?

source.notes: Burton produced and originated the story but was busy directing Batman Returns.

questions.q: What animation technique was used to bring the film to life?

source.notes: About 109,440 frames shot over three years.

questions.q: What title does the main character Jack Skellington hold?

source.notes: Ruler of Halloween Town.

questions.q: Who wrote the original poem that inspired the film?

source.notes: Burton wrote the poem in 1982 while working at Disney.

questions.q: What is the name of Jack Skellington's rag-doll love interest?

source.notes: Stitched-together creation of Dr. Finkelstein.

questions.q: Who composed the film's music and performs Jack's singing voice?

source.notes: Elfman co-wrote the songs with Burton.

questions.q: Which sack-bodied, bug-filled villain terrorizes Halloween Town?

source.notes: A gambling man made of insects under a burlap sack.

questions.q: In what year was The Nightmare Before Christmas released?

source.notes: Premiered at the New York Film Festival.

questions.q: Under which Disney label was the film originally released in 1993?

source.notes: Disney thought it was too scary for the core Disney brand.

questions.q: What is the name of Jack Skellington's loyal ghost dog?

source.notes: Has a glowing jack-o'-lantern nose.

questions.q: Which actor provides Jack Skellington's speaking voice?

source.notes: Also known as Prince Humperdinck in The Princess Bride.

questions.q: Who voices Sally, the ragdoll creation of Doctor Finkelstein?

source.notes: Later a staple of Burton collaborations and Schitt's Creek.

questions.q: What is the opening musical number that introduces Halloween Town?

source.notes: Later covered by Marilyn Manson and Panic! at the Disco.

questions.q: Which song does Jack sing as he wonders at the sights of Christmas Town?

source.notes: Jack discovers snow, lights, stockings, and cookies.

questions.q: How does Jack mispronounce the name 'Santa Claus'?

source.notes: Repeated by Halloween Town characters throughout the film.

questions.q: Which Cab Calloway song inspired the style of Oogie Boogie's musical number?

source.original_a: Cab Calloway's St. James Infirmary Blues
source.notes: Elfman cited Calloway as a key influence.

questions.q: What are the names of the three masked trick-or-treater henchmen who kidnap Santa?

source.notes: Oogie Boogie's dutiful deputies.

questions.q: Who voices Doctor Finkelstein, the mad scientist who built Sally?

source.notes: Known for Prizzi's Honor (1985).

questions.q: Approximately how many puppets were built for the film's stop-motion production?

source.notes: Jack alone had around 400 interchangeable heads.

questions.q: Which actor voices the villain Oogie Boogie?

source.notes: A Broadway performer (Cats, Ain't Misbehavin').

questions.q: Which Pee-wee's Playhouse star voices Lock, one of Oogie Boogie's trick-or-treater henchmen?

source.notes: Reubens also starred in Burton's Pee-wee's Big Adventure (1985).

questions.q: What is the runtime of The Nightmare Before Christmas?

source.notes: Short for a feature film; reflects the painstaking stop-motion schedule.
